SQL> VAR v_clob CLOB SQL> BEGIN dbms_workload_replay.compare_period_report(replay_id1 => 4, replay_id2 => null, format => DBMS_WORKLOAD_REPLAY.TYPE_HTML, result => :v_clob); 2 END; 3 / PL/SQL procedure successfully completed. SQL> SQL> print v_clob; Compare Period Report
Compare Period Report: Capture vs. Replay

Collapse all sections Expand all sections

This report compares the performance of a workload replay against the per formance of the original captured system. Throughout the report "Capture&qu ot; refers to the original captured system, while "Replay" refers to t he replayed workload. The most reliable experiment would compare two replays. Th e first replay would try to mimic the captured system as much as possible withou t any system changes. The second replay would be similar to the first while appl ying a single change as the test variable. (Test systems are almost always an ap proximation of production. The idea in comparing two replays is to isolate the c hange we want to apply and thus assess the effect of such a change on a system s imilar to production.)

(+) (-) General Information

This section describes the experimental setup. Check it carefully to verify that the intended experiment was performed.

(+) (-) Data Sources

Capture Replay
Capture/Replay name capture_001 ratpoc_time
Capture/Replay ID 1 4
Data Directory Name RPLY_DIR RPLY_DIR
Directory Path /anfvol/rat_poc/db_replay/capture_001 /anfvol/rat_poc/db_replay/capture_001
PL/SQL Mode TOP_LEVEL TOP_LEVEL
Encryption Algorithm

(+) (-) Information About Databases

Capture Replay
Database Name AEB AEB
Original Database ID 1779510129 1779510129
Version 19.0.0.0.0 19.0.0.0.0
Platform Linux x86 64-bit Linux x86 64-bit
Is It RAC? NO NO
Number of Instances 1 1

(+) (-) Information About AWR and Time Periods

Capture Replay
Start Time 2022-01-20 08:20:37 2022-01-24 10:06:11
End Time 2022-01-20 08:50:55 2022-01-24 11:42:23
Duration 30.3 minutes 1.6 hours
AWR Database ID 1230158049 1779510129
AWR Start Snapshot ID 12172 12173
AWR End Snapshot ID 12173 12175

(+) (-) Changes to Important Parameters

Capture Replay
sessions 7008 7040

(+) (-) Changes to Optimizer-Relevant Parameters

Capture Replay
cpu_count 24 64

(+) (-) Changes to Memory Configuration Parameters

Capture Replay
db_cache_size 47.38 G 47.5 G

(+) (-) Instances of the Capture Database

instance number instance name host name number of CPU cores number of CPU sockets physical memory instance type
1 AEB isxl1320 12 2 1007.45 G RDBMS

(+) (-) Instances of the Replay Database

instance number instance name host name number of CPU cores number of CPU sockets physical memory instance type
1 AEB azr-eus2l1050 64 1 503.72 G RDBMS

(+) (-) Replay Divergence

This section describes the divergence in replay compared to the capture d system. Please look at the full divergence report if this report shows signifi cant divergence. The possible divergence levels are: (NONE) no divergence detect ed at all (LOW) minimal divergence detected but the performance comparison is mo st likely still valid (MEDIUM) some non-trivial divergence is detected and the p erformance comparison is suspect (HIGH) severe divergence detected and the perfo rmance comparison is unlikely to be informative.

Divergence Level Percent of Calls That Diverged
Replay Divergence (compared to Capture) LOW 0%

(+) (-) Main Performance Statistics

This section does a high-level performance comparison of the two period s. Start by looking for a change in Database Time. If there is no significant c hange in Database Time, you can assume performance as a whole is similar. You ca n look for a change in the Database Time pieces that follow (CPU, User I/O, and Cluster) to see how the different ingredients of Database Time changed from one period to the next, either to explain a change in Database Time or to see if som e pieces regressed and others improved.

Change in DB time Capture total time Replay total time Capture % of DB time Replay % of DB time
Database Time 303.01% 9959.99 seconds 40139.51 seconds 100 100
CPU Time 41.85% 2212.56 seconds 3138.52 seconds 22.21 7.82
User I/O Wait Time 363.7% 7971.99 seconds 36966.11 seconds 80.04 92.09

(+) (-) Top SQL/Call

(+) (-) Top SQL by Change in DB Time

This section compares the performance change of individual SQL statem ents from one period to the next. SQL statements are identified by their force m atching signature to account for literal usage. They are ordered by the total c hange in DB Time, as the most relevant changes are those that impact total throu ghput the most. Any SQL tuning you do should begin with the statement that regre ssed by the most DB Time.

(+) (-) Top Call by Change in DB Time

This section compares the performance change of individual database c alls from one period to the next. A call is identified by File ID and Call Count er. They are ordered by the change in DB Time, as the most relevant changes are those that impact total throughput the most. SQL_ID and SQL text are displayed f or information purpose about the call. Any SQL tuning you do should begin with t he statement that regressed by the most DB Time.

(+) (-) Hardware Usage Comparison

(+) (-) CPU Usage

This section describes general CPU usage on the systems and helps ass ess if they were CPU bound. The number of CPUs is summed over all instances. CPU usage is averaged over instances. Note that "Oracle Run-queue Load" i s for Oracle processes only and usually underestimates the run-queue part.

system CPU Sockets/Cores/Threads Hosts' CPU Usage Oracle Sessions on CPU Oracle Run-queue Load
Capture 2/12/24 8.59% 1.71 active sessions 0 active sessions
Replay 1/64/64 1.4% .92 active sessions 0 active sessions

(+) (-) I/O to Data and Temp Files

This section shows some basic I/O statistics about data and temp file s. First look at the single block read time. An I/O bound system will show a hig h value (much more than 10 miliseconds). The total read/write rates should provi de some guidance to determine if high single-block latencies are caused by an ex cess of requests from the database or poor I/O throughput in general.

Capture Replay
average time of single block read (miliseconds) .11 2.14
total number of single block reads 10860387 14415764
total physical read 105.754 G 209.168 G
total physical write 174.941 G 218.016 G
read rate 59.567M per sec 37.108M per sec
write rate 98.537M per sec 38.678M per sec

(+) (-) ADDM Comparison

This section provides a comparison of ADDM analyses of the two time per iods ordered by the absolute difference in impact. By comparing ADDM results for the two periods, you can see problems that existed in one but not the other, as well as a change in individual finding impacts. If your change is intended to improve database throughput, you should see that improvement reflected in the AD DM findings and impacts.

impact (sec) average active sessions percent active sessions
"User I/O" wait Class

Capture

Replay

7874

36832

4.33

6.38

78.98

91.76
Commits and Rollbacks

Capture

Replay

N/A

860

N/A

.15

N/A

2.14


ASH Data Comparison: Capture[ capture_001 ] Vs Replay[ ratpoc_time ]

NOTE: Time Duration is represented in seconds across this report.

Time Period (1) - capture_001
Time Period (2) - ratpoc_time

Time Desc Time Period1 Time Period2
Begin Time 20-Jan-22 08:20:37 24-Jan-22 10:06:11
End Time 20-Jan-22 08:50:55 24-Jan-22 11:42:23
Collapse all sections Expand all sections

(+) (-) Compare Summary

This section summarizes the activity between the two time periods.

(+) (-) DB Time Distribution

Comparison Metric Time Period1 Time Period2
Total Sample Count 1800 5730
Total DB Time 9960 39960
Total CPU Time 1830 2390
Total WAIT Time 8130 37570
Total IO Time 7960 36800
IO Request Count 1095849 1426411
Avg Time Per IO Request 00000000.00726 00000000.02580

Time Period(1) Time Period(2)
Event(1) Wait time(1) Event Count(1)
db file sequential read,User I/O 7820 1075836
log file sync,Commit 130 8949
db file scattered read,User I/O 120 19289
Sync ASM rebalance,Other 10 47
db file parallel read,User I/O 10 65
direct path read,User I/O 10 659
enq: TX - row lock contention,Application 10 2849
control file sequential read,System I/O 10 4464
PGA memory operation,Other 10 62500
Event(2) Wait time(2) Event Count(2)
db file sequential read,User I/O 35520 1386904
db file scattered read,User I/O 1220 37494
log file sync,Commit 670 13549
control file sequential read,System I/O 90 4239
direct path read,User I/O 50 1780
Sync ASM rebalance,Other 10 29
db file parallel read,User I/O 10 233
